<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The tags are wrapped in an anchor tag within `note-card.html`, making them clickable.

Clicking a tag currently navigates to the note page instead of the tag page. The user likely wants tags to remain non-clickable or to navigate to their own pages.

The user likely means the tags under the title on the single note page should link to tag taxonomy pages, matching blog behavior. This appears to be a regression from the recent whole-card clickability change.

Restructuring the card template is needed to make tags clickable without nesting anchor tags. The single note page should link tags to `/tags/{tag}/`.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Hugo includes tags as a default taxonomy. This likely explains why the tags appear clickable.

Tags in the note single page will link to `/tags/`. List pages and card partials were considered but skipped since the user specifically mentioned the title area.

No shared partial will be created to keep the change minimal. The note single page will mirror the blog tag link pattern, adding href attributes and hover styles to match the existing chip design.

Reconsidering whether "clickable" means tags are incorrectly navigating to the note itself. If the list page uses whole-card links, tags inside the card would be clickable. Both list and single pages place tags beneath the title, so either could be the issue.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Tags are plain spans on note pages — I'll make them link to tag pages like the blog does.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Tags under the note title will be made clickable on the single page.

Reconsidering the user's intent. The tags may actually be clickable because the whole note card is a link. This would be a bug report rather than a feature request.

The list page likely still uses the old title-only link structure. Tags on the single page are currently plain text spans without links. Making them clickable will match the blog layout.

Scope is limited to single.html. Re-examining the user's message to determine whether they want tags made clickable or are reporting that they are currently clickable.

List.html will also get clickable tags for consistency. Nested anchors would be invalid HTML inside note cards, so card links remain unchanged. A shared partial may be extracted to avoid duplication.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
list.html now uses note-card.html. The earlier read was likely cached.

Tags on the list page sit inside the card anchor, so they currently navigate to the note itself. They should instead link to tag taxonomy pages. Single-note tags are separate spans and were not clickable.

The fix requires a stretched-link pattern. The card should become a non-clickable container, the title should link directly, and tags should be separate anchor elements outside the card link.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

List cards wrap tags inside the card link, so tag clicks go to the note. Refactoring so tags link to tag pages while keeping the card clickable.


_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2"><details>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The note card wraps its entire content in an anchor tag, so clicking the tags navigates to the note page rather than behaving as standalone links.

Refactoring note-card.html to use an `<article>` wrapper. The card itself will become a stretched link, while tags will become separate anchor elements positioned above the card via `relative z-1`.

The title link will carry the `stretched-link` class to cover the card body. Tags will sit in a separate list above the card using `relative z-1`.

The title link will carry `data-wikilink-preview` and an `aria-labelledby` reference. The description and shared text will sit as siblings inside the article, letting the stretched link overlay cover the entire card body.

The note-link-card wrapper also needs updating because it nests note-card inside a list item. A shared `notes/tag-chips.html` partial could centralize the tag chip markup for reuse across both list and single-page views.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

```json
  "pattern": "stretched-link"
}
```

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
No stretched-link utility exists in the project. Custom CSS will be added for the notes card pattern.

The note card template was refactored so the title link sits inside the article element. This preserves the card's interactive border styling while keeping the title clickable.

Tag links are now nested inside the article element. An invisible overlay link will cover the entire card to handle clicks on the body area. Tags will sit above the overlay with a higher z-index so they remain clickable.

The overlay will sit at z-0 behind the content. Title and description will be raised to z-1 with pointer-events disabled so clicks pass through to the overlay. Tags will stay above the overlay with a higher z-index so they remain clickable.

The overlay link will use absolute positioning to cover the entire card. Title and description will sit at z-1 with pointer-events disabled so clicks pass through to the overlay. Tag links will sit at z-2 with pointer-events enabled so they remain clickable.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Refactoring the note card so tags link to tag pages instead of inheriting the card’s note link.


_**Agent (default)**_

_**Agent (default)**_

Tags now link to tag pages instead of inheriting the note card’s click target.

**On note pages** (`layouts/notes/single.html`): tags under the title are proper links to `/tags/{tag}/`, same pattern as blog posts.

**On note cards** (`layouts/_partials/notes/note-card.html`): the whole card was one `<a>`, so clicking a tag opened the note. Cards now use a stretched overlay for the note link, with tag chips layered above as their own links.

Shared markup lives in `layouts/_partials/notes/tag-chips.html` so single pages and cards stay consistent. Click a tag → tag archive; click anywhere else on the card → the note.
